briefdescription.para: Create a display resource representation and return its unique RID.
Set display size geometry parameter tokens.

parameterdescription.para: Size of the screen in logical pixels.
Physical width and height of the display in millimeters.

briefdescription.para: Set the active device calibration resource.
Set the active biological calibration resource.
Set window location and viewport transformation tokens.

parameterdescription.para: Position of the window relative to primary screen top-left.

briefdescription.para: Free the display resource.
Create a spatial camera model resource relative to a display.
Set camera offset translation and tilt angle relative to display center-top.

parameterdescription.para: Tilt angle in degrees (positive tilts down).

briefdescription.para: Associate the camera model with a raw VisionServer camera capture stream.
Free the camera model resource.
Create a face tracker resource relative to a camera.

memberdef.argsstring: (RID p_face, Vector3 p_translation, Vector3 p_rotation, bool p_detected)
memberdef.name: face_tracker_set_pose

briefdescription.para: Set the current estimated head pose and detection status.

parameterdescription.para: Head translation vector in Camera Space (mm).
Head rodrigues rotation vector in Camera Space.

briefdescription.para: Free the face tracker resource.
Retrieve head pose parameters in standard camera space.
Create an eye tracker resource relative to a face.
Feed raw estimated gaze parameters in Camera Space and calculate screen intersections.

parameterdescription.para: Gaze origin 3D vector in Camera Space (mm).
Gaze direction 3D unit vector in Camera Space.

briefdescription.para: Set a coordinate smoothing filter resource.
Retrieve eye crop images (left, right) for native UI display previews.
Free the eye tracker resource.
Retrieve gaze origin and direction from eye tracker.

memberdef.argsstring: (RID p_eye, const Ref< Image > &p_left_crop, const Ref< Image > &p_right_crop)
memberdef.name: set_crops_on_eye_tracker

briefdescription.para: Set eye crop preview images.
Reset eye tracker coordinates and signal.
Emit gaze_data_ready for the VisionServer Camera RID.
Retrieve the relative spatial Transform3D for display, camera, face, or eye.
Get the latest calculated gaze coordinate on the display in logical pixels.

parameterdescription.para: True to apply 1 Euro smoothing, false to return raw projection.

briefdescription.para: Query whether a face is actively detected on the tracker.
Update the server's pipeline configuration.
Trigger processing of any pending frames on the main thread (poll results).
Start the background execution pipeline.
Stop the background execution pipeline.
Get the GazeServer global singleton instance.
Singleton coordinating display profiles, camera models, and thread-safe pipeline execution.

detaileddescription.para: GazeServer is the core architectural component of godot-gaze. It manages spatial entities (Displays, Cameras, Faces, Eye Trackers) using RIDs, executes processor pipelines on a background thread, applies calibration, projects gaze rays onto displays, and defer-emits signals to Godot clients.
